comment.body:
I have a related question for Almo or anyone who understands.
I understand that spot/vol beta is positive below the zero vanna zone. But how should I interpret "To break below this lvl volatility must be cooled"?
Does this mean MMs need to sell vol to push the spot price lower? Isn't it that the spot price movement drives vol changes, rather than the opposite?
comment.raw_body:
I have a related question for Almo or anyone who understands.
I understand that spot/vol beta is positive below the zero vanna zone. But how should I interpret "To break below this lvl volatility must be cooled"?
Does this mean MMs need to sell vol to push the spot price lower? Isn't it that the spot price movement drives vol changes, rather than the opposite?

comment.body: In short: which is the driver, vol or spot?
comment.raw_body: In short: which is the driver, vol or spot?

comment.body:
In a long speed zone and spot goes up,
Vanna effect: spot/vol beta is negative, so the vol goes down, which means it's suppressive.
Gamma effect: there will be more GEX which cause MMs to hedge opposite, so it's suppressive as well.

(again, I'm not 100% certain. Correct me if I'm wrong)
comment.raw_body:

comment.body: logically thinking vanna flip doesn’t mean speed goes negative, it means VANNA goes negative. Speed is speed.It’s just the rate of gamma change due to spot. If vol is high when spot flips from positive to negative, delta would dump hard due to the inverse reaction, and MMs would buy back = support
comment.raw_body: logically thinking vanna flip doesn’t mean speed goes negative, it means VANNA goes negative. Speed is speed.It’s just the rate of gamma change due to spot. If vol is high when spot flips from positive to negative, delta would dump hard due to the inverse reaction, and MMs would buy back = support

comment.body: from what I get is speed isn’t inherently suppressive or supportive. Negative speed just means gamma drops more on the way down than it rises on the way up, and positive speed means the opposite = more gamma up than down. Also, vol and gamma move inversely
comment.raw_body: from what I get is speed isn’t inherently suppressive or supportive. Negative speed just means gamma drops more on the way down than it rises on the way up, and positive speed means the opposite = more gamma up than down. Also, vol and gamma move inversely
